title: Iran–US Strait of Hormuz
context: April 2026: a fragile two-week US-Iran ceasefire is collapsing as the IRGC Navy closes the Strait of Hormuz to all transit after Iran's foreign minister had declared it open. Talks resume amid a contested Iranian succession (Supreme Leader reportedly incapacitated; IRGC consolidating control), reimposed EU/UN snapback sanctions, a US naval presence enforcing freedom of navigation, and post-strike uncertainty about Iran's 60%-enriched uranium stockpile.

parties.name: Iran
parties.endState: A written, verifiable permanent halt to US/Israeli strikes; reversal of the Sept 2025 EU/UN snapback and de-listing of CBI/NIOC; implicit recognition of the post-Khamenei leadership.
parties.redLine: No full dismantlement of the nuclear program or capitulation that endangers regime survival; retain a civilian enrichment capability.
parties.batna: Keep the Strait of Hormuz closed and use asymmetric IRGC escalation as leverage, absorbing sanctions with Chinese/Russian help.

parties.name: US
parties.endState: Verified elimination of all 60%-enriched uranium (export/downblend), full IAEA Additional-Protocol restoration with cameras, and a demonstrated Iranian stand-down reopening Hormuz for 30 consecutive days.
parties.redLine: No Iranian nuclear-weapons capability and the Strait must stay open to commercial transit; preserve snapback sanctions as enforcement leverage.
parties.batna: Naval blockade to force the strait open, step-up of sanctions enforcement, and the credible threat of renewed military strikes alongside Israel.
